Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a list of events 18 events with various attributes timestamp activity resource department patient age patient gender additional info We need to infer which events belong to the same case that is same patient journey There is no explicit case ID We need to group events into separate cases based on logical consistency events for a particular patient should have consistent patient attributes age gender and should form a plausible sequence of activities for a single patients episode Also timestamps should be consistent with ordering within a case but they are interleaved across cases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have three patients or perhaps more that appear Patient with age 45 M Patient with age 62 F and Patient with age 28 M But note Events might have same age gender but could be different patients Since age and gender alone are not unique but in a small log likely they correspond to distinct cases

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However there is also department some events are in ER some in Surgery etc But a single patient might move between departments So we need to assign events to cases based on consistency of attributes and plausible flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: It appears we have three distinct sets based on age gender 45M 62F 28M But we must check if any events have the same age gender but belong to different patients Could there be two different 45M patients Possibly but unlikely given the scenario the events seem to form three separate storylines

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 28M seems to be a surgical patient 45M appears to be an ER patient who needed X ray and got diagnosis of hairline fracture then discharged from ER 62F is ER patient with lab test diagnosis minor infection prescription and discharge So clearly three cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case A 45M events 1 4 7 12 15 all share 45M and are consistent Register patient Initial triage X ray imaging Doctor consultation Patient discharged

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case B 62F events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Initial triage for 62F in ER lab requested lab completed doctor consult prescription discharge Yes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case C 28M events 3 6 9 13 16 18 Doctor consultation in Surgery for 28M with reason scheduled knee surgery fits the surgical case Surgery preparation Surgery performed Recovery room Discharge from Surgery So that case is coherent

Name: Validation - PE
Text: That sums to 5 plus 7 plus 6 equals 18 Good

Name: Validation - PE
Text: One might also note that there is a Doctor Lee who appears for both 28M event 6 and event 13 and for 45M event 12 That is okay same doctor can treat multiple patients Also Dr Smith appears for 62F event 8 event 11 event 14 So consistent

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus answer three cases Group A events 1 4 7 12 15 Group B events 2 5 8 10 11 14 17 Group C events 3 6 9 13 16 18
